Section 1  
The function will process a list of participants, each with an age group and a list of health improvements.  

Section 2  
The function will count the frequency of each health improvement for each age group.  

Section 3  
The function will return a dictionary where each key is an age group and the value is the most common health improvement.  

Section 4  
The function will ensure that the output is structured as a dictionary with the most common improvement per age group.  

Section 5  
The function will handle multiple entries per participant and aggregate the improvements across all participants.  

Section 6  
The function will use Python's `collections.Counter` to efficiently count the occurrences of each improvement.  

Section 7  
The function will return the final result in the expected format, ensuring accuracy and clarity.  

* The function processes the input list of participants and aggregates the health improvements per age group.  
* The function returns the most common health improvement for each age group in a dictionary format.
